{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Executive",
  "description": "Jorax Healthcare Limited is a fast-growing Nigerian pharmaceutical, food, and agribusiness company committed to quality, integrity, and long-term impact. We produce and distribute premium products such as Jorax Palm Oil, processed the native way under strict quality standards and certified for safety. Our operations span healthcare, food production, and integrated farming.

Our vision is to improve lives by providing safe, high-quality food and health-focused products while building sustainable businesses that create long-term value.

Our mission is to deliver trusted products through ethical sourcing, disciplined production, and uncompromising quality standards.

At Jorax, we believe in building people as much as building brands. We foster a values-driven environment where excellence, transparency, innovation, and integrity guide everything we do&mdash;creating a workplace where purpose meets performance and every team member can grow, lead, and make real impact.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Executive

Job Location: DeiDei, Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	We are looking to open a physical store for Jorax Palm Oil in Dei Dei market Abuja and we need an experienced sales person to man the store.
	The ideal applicant should reside in Deidei area and know the market very well.
	You will be required to assist in locating, citing the shop and setting it up within a stipulated time.


Qualifications


	Interested candidates should possess an OND / NCE / HND / Bachelor`s Degree with 1 - 5 years of experience.


Salary
N70,000 - N100,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and application letter to: joraxrecruitment@gmail.com using &quot;Sales Executive - DeiDei Abuja&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Sales Executive Job at Jorax Healthcare Limited




        
    

        
        Jorax Healthcare Limited is a fast-growing Nigerian pharmaceutical, food, and agribusiness company committed to quality, integrity, and long-term impact. We produce and distribute premium products such as Jorax Palm Oil, processed the native way under strict quality standards and certified for safety. Our operations span healthcare, food production, and integrated farming.

Our vision is to improve lives by providing safe, high-quality food and health-focused products while building sustainable businesses that create long-term value.

Our mission is to deliver trusted products through ethical sourcing, disciplined production, and uncompromising quality standards.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



At Jorax, we believe in building people as much as building brands. We foster a values-driven environment where excellence, transparency, innovation, and integrity guide everything we do—creating a workplace where purpose meets performance and every team member can grow, lead, and make real impact.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Executive

Job Location: DeiDei, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	We are looking to open a physical store for Jorax Palm Oil in Dei Dei market Abuja and we need an experienced sales person to man the store.
	The ideal applicant should reside in Deidei area and know the market very well.
	You will be required to assist in locating, citing the shop and setting it up within a stipulated time.


Qualifications


	Interested candidates should possess an OND / NCE / HND / Bachelor`s Degree with 1 - 5 years of experience.


Salary
N70,000 - N100,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and application letter to: [email protected] using "Sales Executive - DeiDei Abuja" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at Zetile Stores Limited
content:
Zetile Stores Limited is a fast-growing, 24-hour supermarket chain based in Lekki, Lagos, committed to delivering a seamless, premium retail experience to customers at every hour of the day. As part of the Zetile Group, the Store is built on the values of reliability, quality, and exceptional customer service setting it apart as a trusted neighborhood destination on the Island.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Sales Representative

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Attend to customers on the shop floor promptly, courteously, and professionally, providing accurate product information and guiding purchasing decisions.
	Actively drive in-store sales by promoting products, upselling, and ensuring customers are aware of available options, offers, and new stock.
	Maintain full product knowledge across all categories on the shop floor; stay updated on new arrivals, promotions, and pricing changes.
	Ensure shelves, display units, and product sections are fully stocked, neatly arranged, and properly labelled at all timesincluding during overnight shifts.
	Monitor shelf stock levels continuously and escalate replenishment needs to the Inventory Officer or Supervisor promptly to prevent empty shelves.
	Ensure all displayed products are correctly priced and that shelf labels match POS pricing; report any discrepancies immediately.
	Handle customer enquiries, complaints, and feedback professionally; escalate unresolved issues to the Supervisor without delay.
	Conduct regular shelf checks to identify expired, near-expiry, damaged, or misplaced products and report immediately for removal or action.
	Support stock counts, periodic audits, and store-wide inventory exercises as directed.
	Maintain a clean, organised, and visually appealing shop floor environment throughout every shift.
	Enforce store policies on the floor including orderly conduct, product handling, and loss prevention awareness.
	Prepare and submit accurate end-of-shift floor activity reports to the Supervisor.
	Comply with all store operational procedures, safety standards, and shift schedules including nights, weekends, and public holidays.


Requirements


	Minimum qualification of an SSCE
	Must reside in Lekki, Ajah, or immediate environs OR be willing to take up staff accommodation where available.
	This is a strict and non-negotiable requirement.
	Warm, friendly, and confident customer-facing manner; ability to engage customers professionally at all times.
	Good spoken and written English; clear and respectful communication is required.
	Smart, neat, and professional appearance must be maintained throughout every shift.
	Basic numerical literacy; ability to read price labels, check quantities, and identify discrepancies.
	Prior experience in a retail, sales, or customer service environment is a strong advantage.
	Energetic, physically active, and comfortable being on the shop floor for extended periods.
	Reliable, punctual, and willing to work rotating shifts including nights, weekends, and public holidays in a 24-hour operation.
	A team player with a proactive attitude and genuine willingness to support colleagues across functions.
	High personal integrity; responsible handling of products and honest conduct at all times.


What We Offer


	Net monthly salary: N70,000 during the 3-month probation period.
	Staff accommodation available.
	Upon confirmation of employment: Pension contribution (PENCOM-compliant) and enrolment in the National Housing Fund (NHF).
	Performance recognition and a structured growth pathway.
	A trustworthy, professional, and supportive team environment.
	The opportunity to build a lasting retail career within a growing Nigerian brand.
	Must be open to resume immediately.
	Accomodation available.


Application Closing Date: 5th July, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "SALES REPRESENTATIVE" as the subject of the email.

 

2.) Store Supervisor

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	We are seeking a driven, articulate, and character-led fresh graduate to join the Zetile Stores team as a Store Supervisor.
	In this role, you will support the day-to-day running of store operations, supervise floor staff during your shift, ensure a clean and well-stocked environment, and serve as the go-to person for customer-facing matters during your rotation.
	This is a hands-on, leadership-track role ideal for a graduate who is eager to learn, grow, and make an immediate contribution.


Key Responsibilties


	Oversee all store operations during your assigned 24-hour shift rotation (1 day on, 1 day off).
	Supervise and coordinate floor staff to ensure smooth operations and customer satisfaction at all times.
	Monitor stock levels, report shortages, and ensure shelves are properly stocked and labelled.
	Handle customer enquiries, complaints, and escalations professionally and promptly.
	Conduct shift handover reports and maintain accurate daily logs of store activity.
	Ensure store cleanliness, safety, and compliance with health and hygiene standards.
	Support cash management, sales reconciliation, and end-of-shift reporting.
	Uphold and model the Zetile Store brand standards at all times.
	Communicate operational updates, issues, and team feedback to management clearly and promptly.
	Assist in onboarding and informal coaching of new store staff.
	Lead and inspire team members to deliver great services at all times to all stakeholders.


Requirements


	A fresh graduate (B.Sc.) in any discipline, class of 2023 to 2025.
	Must reside on the Island (Lekki, Victoria Island, Ikoyi, Ajah, or environs), non-negotiable.
	Exceptional spoken and written English. communication is a core part of this role.
	Demonstrably impeccable character: honesty, integrity, and a strong work ethic.
	Positive attitude toward work, a fast learner, and ability to adapt quickly to new processes.
	Physically fit and able to be on your feet throughout a shift.
	Comfortable working in a structured shift rotation (1 day on, 1 day off on a 24-hour schedule).
	Basic computer literacy; familiarity with POS systems is an advantage.
	Strong interpersonal skills with a genuinely customer-first mindset.


Our values fit:


	At Zetile Stores, we hire for character as much as competence. We are looking for someone who takes ownership, shows up prepared, communicates clearly, and treats every customer and colleague with dignity. If you are disciplined, eager to learn, and want to grow within a retail brand that is setting new standards in Lagos, we want to hear from you.


Compensation and Benefits


	Net monthly salary: N150,000.
	Structured shift schedule ensuring adequate rest days.
	On-the-job training and fast-track growth opportunity within the Zetile Group.


Application Closing Date: 22nd June, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Cover letter and CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

3.) Administrative Assistant

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: NYSC

Job Summary


	We are seeking a proactive and well-presented NYSC Corps Member to serve as an Admin Assistant within the Zetile Group.
	This role provides a valuable opportunity to gain real-world administrative and operational experience within a fast-paced, multi-unit business environment.
	You will provide day-to-day administrative support to the Group, contributing to smooth internal operations while developing practical professional skills under experienced leadership.


Responsibilities


	Provide general administrative support to management and operations teams across units.
	Manage correspondence: drafting, receiving, distributing, and filing emails and physical documents.
	Maintain and organize office records, files, and databases with accuracy and confidentiality.
	Coordinate meetings and appointments; scheduling, setting reminders, and preparing briefing notes.
	Receive and direct visitors, calls, and enquiries in a professional and courteous manner.
	Support procurement tasks such as raising purchase requests, tracking deliveries, and maintaining inventory logs.
	Assist with preparation of reports, presentations, and internal documents as required.
	Provide support during events, activations, or programs organized by the Group.
	Carry out any other administrative duties as assigned by the line manager.


Requirements


	Must be an active NYSC Corps Member deployed or redeployed to Lagos State.
	Must reside on the Island (Lekki, Victoria Island, Ikoyi, Ajah, or environs), non-negotiable.
	B.Sc./HND in Business Administration, Social Sciences, Humanities, or any related discipline.
	Good written and spoken English; clear, professional communication is essential.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint).
	Highly organised, punctual, reliable, and discreet in handling confidential information.
	A positive, can-do attitude with a willingness to learn and take initiative.
	Smart appearance and professional conduct at all times.
	Ability to multi-task and work efficiently in a structured but fast-moving environment.
	Accomodation available
	Female candidates only.


What this role offer you


	This is more than a PPA posting, it is a launchpad. You will work directly alongside experienced professionals and be exposed to the inner workings of a growing Nigerian conglomerate.
	Corps members who perform exceptionally during their service year may be considered for full-time opportunities within the Zetile Group post-NYSC.


Compensation and benefits:


	Net monthly allowance: N70,000 (in addition to federal government NYSC allowance).
	Structured on-the-job training and mentorship.
	Exposure to a professional, multi-sector business environment.
	Certificate of completion and strong professional reference upon successful service.
	Opportunity for conversion to full-time employment based on outstanding performance.


Application Closing Date: 18th June, 2026. 

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Social Media Strategist",
  "description": "Outsource Nigeria is a full-service Human Resources agency, specializing in providing outsourced turnkey manpower solutions to growing companies. Our solution include HR Services, Recruiting, Accounting, IT Support, Business Intelligence, Developers, and Operations including management and field / phone / automated based resources. The core of Outsource Nigeria&rsquo;s success is its people.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Social Media Strategist

Job Location: Lekki Aviation Town, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a creative, data-driven, and highly skilled Social Media Strategist to develop and execute effective social media campaigns that drive brand awareness, engagement, and lead generation.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for shaping the company&rsquo;s online presence across multiple platforms.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement comprehensive social media strategies aligned with business goals.
	Manage and grow company social media accounts (Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, LinkedIn, X, etc.).
	Create engaging content plans, including posts, reels, videos, and campaigns.
	Schedule and publish content consistently across all platforms.
	Monitor social media trends, audience behavior, and competitor activity.
	Run and optimize paid social media advertising campaigns.
	Track, analyze, and report performance metrics and campaign results.
	Engage with followers, respond to messages/comments, and manage online community reputation.
	Collaborate with marketing, sales, and design teams to ensure brand consistency.
	Generate leads and support overall digital marketing objectives.


Requirements


	Minimum of HND / B.Sc. in Marketing, Communications, or related field.
	3-5 years of proven experience as a Social Media Manager or Strategist.
	Strong knowledge of social media platforms, algorithms, and trends.
	Experience with Meta Ads Manager, TikTok Ads, or LinkedIn Campaign Manager is an advantage.
	Strong content creation and copywriting skills.
	Ability to analyze data and optimize campaigns for performance.


Core Competencies:


	Creativity and storytelling ability
	Strong analytical and reporting skills
	Digital marketing knowledge
	Communication and engagement skills
	Branding and content strategy
	Time management and consistency.


Salary &amp; Benefits


	Salary: N450,000 / Month.
	Performance-based bonuses may apply
	HMO/Medical Insurance (if applicable)
	Career growth opportunities
	Exposure to high-impact digital campaigns
	Training and development support.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: jobs@outsourcenigeria.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Social Media Strategist Job at Outsource Nigeria




        
    

        
        Outsource Nigeria is a full-service Human Resources agency, specializing in providing outsourced turnkey manpower solutions to growing companies. Our solution include HR Services, Recruiting, Accounting, IT Support, Business Intelligence, Developers, and Operations including management and field / phone / automated based resources. The core of Outsource Nigeria’s success is its people.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Social Media Strategist

Job Location: Lekki Aviation Town, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a creative, data-driven, and highly skilled Social Media Strategist to develop and execute effective social media campaigns that drive brand awareness, engagement, and lead generation.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for shaping the company’s online presence across multiple platforms.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement comprehensive social media strategies aligned with business goals.
	Manage and grow company social media accounts (Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, LinkedIn, X, etc.).
	Create engaging content plans, including posts, reels, videos, and campaigns.
	Schedule and publish content consistently across all platforms.
	Monitor social media trends, audience behavior, and competitor activity.
	Run and optimize paid social media advertising campaigns.
	Track, analyze, and report performance metrics and campaign results.
	Engage with followers, respond to messages/comments, and manage online community reputation.
	Collaborate with marketing, sales, and design teams to ensure brand consistency.
	Generate leads and support overall digital marketing objectives.


Requirements


	Minimum of HND / B.Sc. in Marketing, Communications, or related field.
	3-5 years of proven experience as a Social Media Manager or Strategist.
	Strong knowledge of social media platforms, algorithms, and trends.
	Experience with Meta Ads Manager, TikTok Ads, or LinkedIn Campaign Manager is an advantage.
	Strong content creation and copywriting skills.
	Ability to analyze data and optimize campaigns for performance.


Core Competencies:


	Creativity and storytelling ability
	Strong analytical and reporting skills
	Digital marketing knowledge
	Communication and engagement skills
	Branding and content strategy
	Time management and consistency.


Salary & Benefits


	Salary: N450,000 / Month.
	Performance-based bonuses may apply
	HMO/Medical Insurance (if applicable)
	Career growth opportunities
	Exposure to high-impact digital campaigns
	Training and development support.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Revenue Growth Strategist",
  "description": "Outsource Nigeria is a full-service Human Resources agency, specializing in providing outsourced turnkey manpower solutions to growing companies. Our solution include HR Services, Recruiting, Accounting, IT Support, Business Intelligence, Developers, and Operations including management and field / phone / automated based resources. The core of Outsource Nigeria&rsquo;s success is its people.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Revenue Growth Strategist

Job Location: Lekki Aviation Town, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Revenue Growth Strategist will oversee the commercial growth framework, aligning sales, digital marketing, partnerships, and customer acquisition strategies toward measurable revenue outcomes.
	You are to align fully with the Growth proposition and lead the journey, and you are to build the following operational structure for the final execution framework.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement revenue growth strategies aligned with business objectives.
	Identify new business opportunities, market segments, and revenue streams.
	Analyze market trends, competitor activities, customer behavior, and sales data to inform growth decisions.
	Collaborate with sales, marketing, operations, and product teams to improve revenue performance.
	Design and optimize pricing, customer retention, and acquisition strategies.
	Build strategic partnerships and business alliances to drive market expansion.
	Monitor key revenue KPIs and prepare regular growth performance reports.
	Recommend improvements to sales funnels, customer journeys, and conversion processes.
	Support go-to-market strategies for new products or service offerings.
	Drive initiatives focused on customer lifetime value, upselling, and cross-selling opportunities.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Economics, Marketing, Finance, or related field.
	5-10 years of proven experience in business strategy, growth, revenue operations, sales strategy, or business development roles.
	Strong analytical and financial modeling skills.
	Experience interpreting data and making business recommendations from insights.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, CRM systems, and business analytics tools.


Core Competencies:


	Strategic thinking and problem-solving.
	Revenue optimization and forecasting.
	Data analysis and reporting.
	Market research and competitor intelligence.
	Customer acquisition and retention strategy.
	Business development and partnership management.
	Strong presentation and communication skills.





Salary &amp; Benefits


	Salary: N800,000 / month.
	Performance-based incentives / bonuses.
	HMO / Medical Insurance.
	Performance Bonus.
	Career Development Opportunities.
	Professional Training Support.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: jobs@outsourcenigeria.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Revenue Growth Strategist Job at Outsource Nigeria




        
    

        
        Outsource Nigeria is a full-service Human Resources agency, specializing in providing outsourced turnkey manpower solutions to growing companies. Our solution include HR Services, Recruiting, Accounting, IT Support, Business Intelligence, Developers, and Operations including management and field / phone / automated based resources. The core of Outsource Nigeria’s success is its people.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Revenue Growth Strategist

Job Location: Lekki Aviation Town, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Revenue Growth Strategist will oversee the commercial growth framework, aligning sales, digital marketing, partnerships, and customer acquisition strategies toward measurable revenue outcomes.
	You are to align fully with the Growth proposition and lead the journey, and you are to build the following operational structure for the final execution framework.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement revenue growth strategies aligned with business objectives.
	Identify new business opportunities, market segments, and revenue streams.
	Analyze market trends, competitor activities, customer behavior, and sales data to inform growth decisions.
	Collaborate with sales, marketing, operations, and product teams to improve revenue performance.
	Design and optimize pricing, customer retention, and acquisition strategies.
	Build strategic partnerships and business alliances to drive market expansion.
	Monitor key revenue KPIs and prepare regular growth performance reports.
	Recommend improvements to sales funnels, customer journeys, and conversion processes.
	Support go-to-market strategies for new products or service offerings.
	Drive initiatives focused on customer lifetime value, upselling, and cross-selling opportunities.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Economics, Marketing, Finance, or related field.
	5-10 years of proven experience in business strategy, growth, revenue operations, sales strategy, or business development roles.
	Strong analytical and financial modeling skills.
	Experience interpreting data and making business recommendations from insights.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, CRM systems, and business analytics tools.


Core Competencies:


	Strategic thinking and problem-solving.
	Revenue optimization and forecasting.
	Data analysis and reporting.
	Market research and competitor intelligence.
	Customer acquisition and retention strategy.
	Business development and partnership management.
	Strong presentation and communication skills.





Salary & Benefits


	Salary: N800,000 / month.
	Performance-based incentives / bonuses.
	HMO / Medical Insurance.
	Performance Bonus.
	Career Development Opportunities.
	Professional Training Support.


Method of Application

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "CRM Manager",
  "description": "Outsource Nigeria is a full-service Human Resources agency, specializing in providing outsourced turnkey manpower solutions to growing companies. Our solution include HR Services, Recruiting, Accounting, IT Support, Business Intelligence, Developers, and Operations including management and field / phone / automated based resources. The core of Outsource Nigeria&rsquo;s success is its people.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: CRM Manager

Job Location: Lekki Aviation Town, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The CRM Manager will oversee customer lifecycle management with her team to set up FreshSales, FreshDesk and Freshservices general automation, reporting intelligence, and CRM strategy implementation across all departments.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead CRM strategy and integration.
	Develop reporting frameworks for management.
	Supervise CRM specialists and sales reporting.
	Monitor lead nurturing systems.
	Improve customer retention and engagement.
	Ensure accurate revenue forecasting.
	Align CRM with digital marketing and sales objectives.
	Develop and implement customer relationship management strategies to improve acquisition, retention, and customer lifetime value.
	Manage and optimize CRM platforms, databases, and automation workflows.
	Analyze customer behavior, segmentation, and campaign performance to drive business decisions.
	Design and execute customer engagement, retention, and loyalty campaigns.
	Collaborate with sales, marketing, customer service, and product teams to improve customer experience.
	Monitor customer feedback, complaints, and service metrics to identify improvement opportunities.
	Generate reports and insights on CRM performance, campaign ROI, and customer trends.
	Ensure data quality, compliance, and integrity within CRM systems.
	Train and support internal teams on CRM best practices and processes.


KPIs


	CRM Adoption Rate.
	Customer Retention.
	Lead Conversion Optimization.
	Reporting Efficiency.
	Revenue Tracking Accuracy.


Requirements


	Minimum of a B.Sc. / HND qualification in Marketing, Business Administration, Mass Communication, Economics, or any related discipline.
	5-10 years of proven experience as a CRM Manager, Customer Success Manager, CRM Administrator, or in a similar role.
	Demonstrable experience in CRM administration, including automation workflows, dashboard reporting, customer data management, and data quality control processes.
	Strong working knowledge of CRM platforms such as Salesforce, HubSpot, Zoho CRM, Microsoft Dynamics, or similar systems.
	Experience using Omni Desk or equivalent customer engagement/customer support platforms.
	Solid understanding of customer segmentation, customer lifecycle management, retention strategies, and marketing automation tools.
	Strong analytical mindset with the ability to interpret customer data, generate reports, and provide actionable business insights.
	Excellent communication, stakeholder management, project management, and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to manage multiple projects, improve customer experience processes, and drive CRM optimization initiatives.


Core Competencies:


	Customer relationship management.
	Data analysis and reporting.
	Marketing automation.
	Team collaboration and leadership.
	Campaign management.
	Strategic thinking and execution.


Salary &amp; Benefits


	Salary: N400,000 / month.
	HMO / Medical Insurance.
	Performance Bonuses.
	Career Growth Opportunities.
	Professional Training &amp; Development.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: jobs@outsourcenigeria.com using the Job Positionas the subject of the email.",

Job Position: CRM Manager

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The CRM Manager will oversee customer lifecycle management with her team to set up FreshSales, FreshDesk and Freshservices general automation, reporting intelligence, and CRM strategy implementation across all departments.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead CRM strategy and integration.
	Develop reporting frameworks for management.
	Supervise CRM specialists and sales reporting.
	Monitor lead nurturing systems.
	Improve customer retention and engagement.
	Ensure accurate revenue forecasting.
	Align CRM with digital marketing and sales objectives.
	Develop and implement customer relationship management strategies to improve acquisition, retention, and customer lifetime value.
	Manage and optimize CRM platforms, databases, and automation workflows.
	Analyze customer behavior, segmentation, and campaign performance to drive business decisions.
	Design and execute customer engagement, retention, and loyalty campaigns.
	Collaborate with sales, marketing, customer service, and product teams to improve customer experience.
	Monitor customer feedback, complaints, and service metrics to identify improvement opportunities.
	Generate reports and insights on CRM performance, campaign ROI, and customer trends.
	Ensure data quality, compliance, and integrity within CRM systems.
	Train and support internal teams on CRM best practices and processes.


KPIs


	CRM Adoption Rate.
	Customer Retention.
	Lead Conversion Optimization.
	Reporting Efficiency.
	Revenue Tracking Accuracy.


Requirements


	Minimum of a B.Sc. / HND qualification in Marketing, Business Administration, Mass Communication, Economics, or any related discipline.
	5-10 years of proven experience as a CRM Manager, Customer Success Manager, CRM Administrator, or in a similar role.
	Demonstrable experience in CRM administration, including automation workflows, dashboard reporting, customer data management, and data quality control processes.
	Strong working knowledge of CRM platforms such as Salesforce, HubSpot, Zoho CRM, Microsoft Dynamics, or similar systems.
	Experience using Omni Desk or equivalent customer engagement/customer support platforms.
	Solid understanding of customer segmentation, customer lifecycle management, retention strategies, and marketing automation tools.
	Strong analytical mindset with the ability to interpret customer data, generate reports, and provide actionable business insights.
	Excellent communication, stakeholder management, project management, and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to manage multiple projects, improve customer experience processes, and drive CRM optimization initiatives.


Core Competencies:


	Customer relationship management.
	Data analysis and reporting.
	Marketing automation.
	Team collaboration and leadership.
	Campaign management.
	Strategic thinking and execution.


Salary & Benefits


	Salary: N400,000 / month.
	HMO / Medical Insurance.
	Performance Bonuses.
	Career Growth Opportunities.
	Professional Training & Development.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Positionas the subject of the email.
